blockingIssues.description: Relay token verification defaults to an empty signing key (process.env['MORS_RELAY_SIGNING_KEY'] ?? ''). If unset, tokens signed with the known empty key can be forged and accepted, violating fail-closed auth expectations.

CLI login signs session tokens with a local randomly generated signing key (loadSigningKey/generateSigningKey/saveSigningKey), but relay verification uses MORS_RELAY_SIGNING_KEY from server env. There is no key coordination path or explicit remediation when these differ, so login-issued tokens are not reliably verifiable by the relay.

nonBlockingIssues.description: CLI help text still says login authenticates with GitHub OAuth device flow, conflicting with the hard-switch to mors-native auth.

Deploy test remediation assertion now allows 'flyctl' as alternative regex match immediately after a contains('flyctl') check, weakening enforcement of explicit install/remediation guidance for the missing-flyctl path (VAL-DEPLOY-002).

appliedUpdates.description: Updated architecture.md to reflect mors-native auth (was incorrectly stating GitHub OAuth Device Flow).
Updated environment.md to document MORS_RELAY_SIGNING_KEY and note that GitHub OAuth placeholders are legacy.
Added flyctl PATH co-location caveat to environment.md (node and flyctl may share /opt/homebrew/bin, breaking naive PATH-pruning test helpers).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update mors-global-worker skill scope to include native-identity-core and onboarding-handle-profile milestones, since it is actively assigned to features in those milestones.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: mors-global-worker SKILL.md line 12 lists only relay-foundation/global-async-messaging/realtime-watch/developer-launch-path; features.json assigns native-identity-core features to mors-global-worker.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance to mors-global-worker skill for stabilization/fix features where the strict 'red test first → green' TDD loop does not apply because failing tests already exist. Workers should be able to mark followedProcedure=true for justified stabilization deviations.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Worker for native-identity-core-fix-deploy-tests-flyctl-env-compat marked followedProcedure=false for a justified stabilization deviation; this pattern will recur for all fix features.
